element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Core movement foundation for all levels
Special movement mechanics enabling unique level navigation
19 consecutive levels with no map level between
Departure from side-scrolling map style to new spatial paradigm

typical_relief_triggers: Level completion returns to map screen
Bonus level breaks in level progression
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Episodic structure with varied pacing: Episode 1 uses map-gated progression allowing breaks; Episode 2 employs consecutive levels for sustained tension; Episode 3 shifts to overhead navigation for new pacing rhythm

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Bonus levels accessible from Episode 1 map level

difficulty_curve.early_game: Episode 1 introduces foundational mechanics (run, jump, weapons) in gated progression via map level
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Monster and puzzle complexity increases; transformation power-ups add spatial variety; Episode 2 maintains pressure through consecutive level design without map breaks
difficulty_curve.late_game: Episode 3 employs overhead map style as significant departure; final progression combines mastered mechanics with new spatial paradigm
notable_spikes: Transition from Episode 1 map-gated style to Episode 2's consecutive levels
Introduction of transformation mechanics requiring new movement vocabulary
Episode 3 overhead map navigation requiring spatial reorientation

notable_level_patterns.description: Episode 1 uses interconnected map level to gate access to individual levels, allowing player agency in routing and breaks between intense sequences
Episode 2 removes map level entirely, replacing it with 19 sequential levels and ending level for sustained tension without reprieve
Special levels requiring phoenix (flying), frog (jumping), or fish (swimming) forms create locked areas accessible only after obtaining power-ups
Episode 3 transitions map level to overhead view perspective, fundamentally altering spatial communication and progression logic
Episode 1 concludes with two finish levels where player is passive, creating narrative resolution without mechanical challenge
